g.text: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct — INT8 on T4 (≤8B open-weight rule)
para_id resets to 1 per doc → entries overwrote each other → only last doc survived. Fixed: composite key "doc_id||para_id"
mid-write kill → corrupted JSON. Fixed: write to .tmp then rename (same as task1)
merge_outputs used para_id alone for dedup
Same local int across docs → wrong unique count (211 → reported as 80). Fixed: composite (doc_id, para_id)
education_dimensions_updated.csv uses semicolons. Fixed: pd.read_csv(..., sep=";")
TAGS_CSV pointed to /kaggle/input/... on local Windows machine. Fix: platform check in config.py
T4 limit: 12 hours — Task 1 takes ~12 hrs total, Task 2 ~18+ hrs
Solution A: resume — checkpoint saves after every para/doc, re-run picks up from last save
Solution B: 3 parallel notebooks — each handles 1/3, merge_outputs.py combines at end
Composite key fix confirmed: unique (doc_id, para_id) pairs = total count
task1_predictions.json merged and verified locally. Uploaded to Kaggle dataset.
Running on 3 Kaggle notebooks in parallel. Checkpointing per document. Resume on timeout.
After all 3 Task 2 chunks finish → merge_outputs.py → validate_submission.py → submit
1. Download task2_checkpoint_chunk{N}.json from Kaggle output panel
2. Upload as new version of argmining2026-task2-checkpoints dataset
3. In new notebook: copy task1_predictions.json + checkpoint into outputs/
5. Script prints "X documents already done" and continues from there
6. Repeat until all 29-30 docs per chunk are done
Submission deadline: 1 April 2026 · Workshop: 3 July 2026, San Diego
